aberration.name: Aberration
aberration.summary: Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.
aberration.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.

abjuration.name: Abjuration
abjuration.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the abjuration school of magic, typically involving protection or wards.
abjuration.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the abjuration school of magic, typically involving protection or wards.

acid.name: Acid
acid.summary: Effects with this trait deal acid damage. Creatures with this trait have a magical connection to acid.
acid.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Effects with this trait deal acid damage. Creatures with this trait have a magical connection to acid.

additive.name: Additive
additive.summary: Feats with the additive trait allow you to spend actions to add special substances to  bombs  or  elixirs . You can add only one additive to a single …
additive.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 75

Feats with the additive trait allow you to spend actions to add special substances to bombs or elixirs. You can add only one additive to a single alchemical item, and attempting to add another spoils the item. You can typically use actions with the additive trait only when you're creating an infused alchemical item, and some can be used only with the Quick Alchemy action. The additive trait is always followed by a level, such as additive 2. An additive adds its level to the level of the alchemical item you're modifying; the result is the new level of the mixture. The mixture's item level must be no higher than your advanced alchemy level.

air.name: Air
air.summary: Effects with the air trait either manipulate or conjure air. Those that manipulate air have no effect in a vacuum or an area without air. Creatures …
air.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Effects with the air trait either manipulate or conjure air. Those that manipulate air have no effect in a vacuum or an area without air. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of air or have a magical connection to that element. Planes with this trait consist mostly of open spaces and air with various degrees of turbulence, though they also contain rare islands of floating stone and other elements and energies.

alchemical.name: Alchemical
alchemical.summary: Alchemical items are powered by reactions of alchemical reagents. Alchemical items aren’t magical and don’t radiate a magical aura.
alchemical.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Alchemical items are powered by reactions of alchemical reagents. Alchemical items aren’t magical and don’t radiate a magical aura.

alchemist.name: Alchemist
alchemist.summary: This indicates abilities from the  alchemist  class.
alchemist.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

This indicates abilities from the alchemist class.

angel.name: Angel
angel.summary: This family of  celestials  is native to the plane of  Nirvana . Most angels are neutral good, have  darkvision , and have a weakness to evil damage.
angel.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

This family of celestials is native to the plane of Nirvana. Most angels are neutral good, have darkvision, and have a weakness to evil damage.

animal.name: Animal
animal.summary: An animal is a creature with a relatively low intelligence. It typically doesn’t have an Intelligence ability modifier over –4, can’t speak …
animal.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

An animal is a creature with a relatively low intelligence. It typically doesn’t have an Intelligence ability modifier over –4, can’t speak languages, and can’t be trained in Intelligence-based skills.

apex.name: Apex
apex.summary: When you Invest an Item that has the apex trait, it improves one of your ability scores, either increasing it by 2 or to a total of 18, whichever …
apex.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

When you Invest an Item that has the apex trait, it improves one of your ability scores, either increasing it by 2 or to a total of 18, whichever grants the higher score. This gives you all the benefits of the new ability score until the investiture ends. An apex item grants this benefit only the first time it’s invested within a 24-hour period, and you can benefit from only one apex item at a time. If you attempt to invest an apex item when you already have one invested, you don’t gain the ability score increase, though you do gain any other effects of Investing the Item.

arcane.name: Arcane
arcane.summary: This magic comes from the arcane tradition, which is built on logic and rationality. Anything with this trait is  magical .
arcane.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

This magic comes from the arcane tradition, which is built on logic and rationality. Anything with this trait is magical.

archetype.name: Archetype
archetype.summary: This feat belongs to an archetype.
archetype.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

This feat belongs to an archetype.

archon.name: Archon
archon.summary: Members of this family of  celestials  are the protectors of  Heaven  and are lawful good. They have  darkvision  and a weakness to evil damage.
archon.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Members of this family of celestials are the protectors of Heaven and are lawful good. They have darkvision and a weakness to evil damage.

astral.name: Astral
astral.summary: Astral creatures are native of the  Astral Plane . They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Astral Plane.
astral.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 628

Astral creatures are native of the Astral Plane. They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Astral Plane.

attack.name: Attack
attack.summary: An ability with this trait involves an attack. For each attack you make beyond the first on your turn, you take a multiple attack penalty.
attack.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

An ability with this trait involves an attack. For each attack you make beyond the first on your turn, you take a multiple attack penalty.

auditory.name: Auditory
auditory.summary: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can …
auditory.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can't hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

azata.name: Azata
azata.summary: This family of  celestials  is native to  Elysium . They are typically chaotic good and have  darkvision  and a weakness to evil and  cold iron .
azata.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

This family of celestials is native to Elysium. They are typically chaotic good and have darkvision and a weakness to evil and cold iron.

barbarian.name: Barbarian
barbarian.summary: This indicates abilities from the  barbarian  class.
barbarian.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

This indicates abilities from the barbarian class.

bard.name: Bard
bard.summary: This indicates abilities from the  bard  class.
bard.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

This indicates abilities from the bard class.

beast.name: Beast
beast.summary: A creature similar to an  animal  but with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher is usually a beast. Unlike an animal, a beast might be able to …
beast.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

A creature similar to an animal but with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher is usually a beast. Unlike an animal, a beast might be able to speak and reason.

bomb.name: Bomb
bomb.summary: An alchemical bomb combines volatile alchemical components that explode when the bomb hits a creature or object. Most alchemical bombs deal damage, …
bomb.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

An alchemical bomb combines volatile alchemical components that explode when the bomb hits a creature or object. Most alchemical bombs deal damage, though some produce other effects.

cantrip.name: Cantrip
cantrip.summary: A spell you can cast at will that is automatically heightened to half your level rounded up.
cantrip.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

A spell you can cast at will that is automatically heightened to half your level rounded up.

celestial.name: Celestial
celestial.summary: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the good-aligned planes are called celestials. Celestials can survive the basic environmental …
celestial.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the good-aligned planes are called celestials. Celestials can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

champion.name: Champion
champion.summary: This indicates abilities from the  champion  class.
champion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

This indicates abilities from the champion class.

chaotic.name: Chaotic
chaotic.summary: Chaotic effects often manipulate energy from chaos-aligned Outer Planes and are anathema to lawful divine servants or divine servants of lawful …
chaotic.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

Chaotic effects often manipulate energy from chaos-aligned Outer Planes and are anathema to lawful divine servants or divine servants of lawful deities. A creature with this trait is chaotic in alignment.

cleric.name: Cleric
cleric.summary: This indicates abilities from the  cleric  class.
cleric.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

This indicates abilities from the cleric class.

cold.name: Cold
cold.summary: Effects with this trait deal cold damage. Creatures with this trait have a connection to magical cold.
cold.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

Effects with this trait deal cold damage. Creatures with this trait have a connection to magical cold.

common.name: Common
common.summary: Anything that doesn't list another rarity trait ( uncommon ,  rare , or  unique ) automatically has the common trait. This rarity indicates that an …
common.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

Anything that doesn't list another rarity trait (uncommon, rare, or unique) automatically has the common trait. This rarity indicates that an ability, item, or spell is available to all players who meet the prerequisites for it. A creature of this rarity is generally known and can be summoned with the appropriate _summon_ spell.

companion.name: Companion
companion.summary: An item with this trait can be worn by an  animal companion  or similar creature. A companion can have up to two items invested.
companion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

An item with this trait can be worn by an animal companion or similar creature. A companion can have up to two items invested.

complex.name: Complex
complex.summary: A hazard with this trait takes turns in an encounter.
complex.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

A hazard with this trait takes turns in an encounter.

composition.name: Composition
composition.summary: To cast a composition cantrip or focus spell, you usually use a type of  Performance . If the spell includes a verbal component, you must use an  …
composition.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 629

To cast a composition cantrip or focus spell, you usually use a type of Performance. If the spell includes a verbal component, you must use an auditory performance, and if it includes a somatic component, you must use a visual one. The spell gains all the traits of the performance you used. You can cast only one composition spell each turn, and you can have only one active at a time. If you cast a new composition spell, any ongoing effects from your previous composition spell end immediately.

concentrate.name: Concentrate
concentrate.summary: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.
concentrate.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

conjuration.name: Conjuration
conjuration.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the conjuration school of magic, typically involving  summoning , creation,  …
conjuration.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the conjuration school of magic, typically involving summoning, creation, teleportation, or moving things from place to place.

consecration.name: Consecration
consecration.summary: A consecration spell enhances an area for an extended period of time. A given area can have only a single consecration effect at a time. The new …
consecration.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

A consecration spell enhances an area for an extended period of time. A given area can have only a single consecration effect at a time. The new effect attempts to counteract any existing one in areas of overlap.

construct.name: Construct
construct.summary: A construct is an artificial creature empowered by a force other than  necromancy . Constructs are often  mindless ; they are immune to bleed damage, …
construct.description:
**Source** Bestiary 2 pg. 346

A construct is an artificial creature empowered by a force other than necromancy. Constructs are often mindless; they are immune to bleed damage, death effects, disease, healing, necromancy, nonlethal attacks, poison, and the doomed, drained, fatigued, paralyzed, sickened, and unconscious conditions; and they may have Hardness based on the materials used to construct their bodies. Constructs are not living creatures, nor are they undead. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, a construct creature is destroyed.

consumable.name: Consumable
consumable.summary: An item with this trait can be used only once. Unless stated otherwise, it's destroyed after activation. Consumable items include  alchemical items  …
consumable.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

An item with this trait can be used only once. Unless stated otherwise, it's destroyed after activation. Consumable items include alchemical items and magical consumables such as scrolls and talismans. When a character creates consumable items, they can make them in batches of four.

contact.name: Contact
contact.summary: A contact  poison  is activated by applying it to an item or directly onto a living creature's skin. The first creature to touch the affected item …
contact.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 550

A contact poison is activated by applying it to an item or directly onto a living creature's skin. The first creature to touch the affected item must attempt a saving throw against the poison; if the poison is applied directly, the creature must attempt a saving throw immediately when the poison touches its skin. Contact poisons are infeasible to apply to a creature via a weapon attack due to the logistics of delivering them without poisoning yourself. Typically, the onset time of a contact poison is 1 minute.

curse.name: Curse
curse.summary: A curse is an effect that places some long-term affliction on a creature. Curses are always  magical  and are typically the result of a spell or …
curse.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

A curse is an effect that places some long-term affliction on a creature. Curses are always magical and are typically the result of a spell or trap. Effects with this trait can be removed only by effects that specifically target curses. 

Afflictions with this trait are manifestations of potent ill will. A curse either lasts a specified amount of time or can be removed only by certain actions a character must perform or conditions they must meet. A curse with stages follows the rules for afflictions. (_Gamemastery Guide_ pg. 251)

darkness.name: Darkness
darkness.summary: Darkness effects extinguish non-magical light in the area, and can  counteract  less powerful magical  light . You must usually target light magic …
darkness.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

Darkness effects extinguish non-magical light in the area, and can counteract less powerful magical light. You must usually target light magic with your darkness magic directly to counteract the light, but some darkness spells automatically attempt to counteract light.

death.name: Death
death.summary: An effect with the death trait kills you immediately if it reduces you to 0 HP. Some death effects can bring you closer to death or slay you outright …
death.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

An effect with the death trait kills you immediately if it reduces you to 0 HP. Some death effects can bring you closer to death or slay you outright without reducing you to 0 HP.

dedication.name: Dedication
dedication.summary: You must select a feat with this trait to apply an archetype to your character.
dedication.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

You must select a feat with this trait to apply an archetype to your character.

demon.name: Demon
demon.summary: A family of  fiends , demons hail from or trace their origins to the  Abyss . Most are irredeemably chaotic evil and have  darkvision .
demon.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

A family of fiends, demons hail from or trace their origins to the Abyss. Most are irredeemably chaotic evil and have darkvision.

detection.name: Detection
detection.summary: Effects with this trait attempt to determine the presence or location of a person, object, or aura.
detection.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

Effects with this trait attempt to determine the presence or location of a person, object, or aura.

devil.name: Devil
devil.summary: A family of  fiends  from  Hell , most devils are irredeemably lawful evil. They typically have  greater darkvision , immunity to fire, and  …
devil.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

A family of fiends from Hell, most devils are irredeemably lawful evil. They typically have greater darkvision, immunity to fire, and telepathy.

dinosaur.name: Dinosaur
dinosaur.summary: These reptiles have survived from prehistoric times.
dinosaur.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 630

These reptiles have survived from prehistoric times.

disease.name: Disease
disease.summary: An effect with this trait applies one or more diseases. A disease is typically an affliction.
disease.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

An effect with this trait applies one or more diseases. A disease is typically an affliction.

divination.name: Divination
divination.summary: The divination school of magic typically involves obtaining or transferring information, or predicting events.
divination.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

The divination school of magic typically involves obtaining or transferring information, or predicting events.

divine.name: Divine
divine.summary: This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is  magical .
divine.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.

downtime.name: Downtime
downtime.summary: An activity with this trait takes a day or more, and can be used only during downtime.
downtime.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

An activity with this trait takes a day or more, and can be used only during downtime.

dragon.name: Dragon
dragon.summary: Dragons are reptilian creatures, often winged or with the power of flight. Most are able to use a breath weapon and are immune to  sleep  and  …
dragon.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Dragons are reptilian creatures, often winged or with the power of flight. Most are able to use a breath weapon and are immune to sleep and paralysis.

drow.name: Drow
drow.summary: Subterranean kin of the  elves , drow typically have  darkvision  and inborn magical abilities.
drow.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Subterranean kin of the elves, drow typically have darkvision and inborn magical abilities.

druid.name: Druid
druid.summary: This indicates abilities from the  druid  class.
druid.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

This indicates abilities from the druid class.

duergar.name: Duergar
duergar.summary: Subterranean kin of the  dwarves , duergar typically have  darkvision  and immunity to  poison . They are not easily fooled by  illusions .
duergar.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Subterranean kin of the dwarves, duergar typically have darkvision and immunity to poison. They are not easily fooled by illusions.

dwarf.name: Dwarf
dwarf.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the dwarf ancestry. Dwarves are stout folk who often live underground and typically have  darkvision . An …
dwarf.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

A creature with this trait is a member of the dwarf ancestry. Dwarves are stout folk who often live underground and typically have darkvision. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by dwarves. An item with this trait is created and used by dwarves.

earth.name: Earth
earth.summary: Effects with the earth trait either manipulate or conjure earth. Those that manipulate earth have no effect in an area without earth. Creatures with …
earth.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Effects with the earth trait either manipulate or conjure earth. Those that manipulate earth have no effect in an area without earth. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of earth or have a magical connection to that element. Planes with this trait are mostly solid, with caverns and other hollow pockets.

electricity.name: Electricity
electricity.summary: Effects with this trait deal electricity damage. A creature with this trait has a magical connection to electricity.
electricity.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Effects with this trait deal electricity damage. A creature with this trait has a magical connection to electricity.

elemental.name: Elemental
elemental.summary: Elementals are creatures directly tied to an element and native to the Elemental Planes. Elementals don’t need to breathe.
elemental.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Elementals are creatures directly tied to an element and native to the Elemental Planes. Elementals don’t need to breathe.

elf.name: Elf
elf.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the elf ancestry. Elves are mysterious people with rich traditions of magic and scholarship who typically …
elf.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

A creature with this trait is a member of the elf ancestry. Elves are mysterious people with rich traditions of magic and scholarship who typically have low-light vision. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by elves. A weapon with this trait is created and used by elves.

elixir.name: Elixir
elixir.summary: Elixirs are  alchemical  liquids that are used by drinking them.
elixir.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Elixirs are alchemical liquids that are used by drinking them.

emotion.name: Emotion
emotion.summary: This effect alters a creature's emotions. Effects with this trait always have the  mental  trait as well. Creatures with special training or that …
emotion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

This effect alters a creature's emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

enchantment.name: Enchantment
enchantment.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the enchantment school of magic, typically involving mind control, emotion alteration, …
enchantment.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the enchantment school of magic, typically involving mind control, emotion alteration, and other mental effects.

environmental.name: Environmental
environmental.summary: A hazard with this trait is something dangerous that’s part of the natural world, such as quicksand or harmful mold.
environmental.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

A hazard with this trait is something dangerous that’s part of the natural world, such as quicksand or harmful mold.

ethereal.name: Ethereal
ethereal.summary: Ethereal creatures are natives of the  Ethereal Plane . They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Ethereal Plane.
ethereal.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Ethereal creatures are natives of the Ethereal Plane. They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Ethereal Plane.

evil.name: Evil
evil.summary: Evil effects often manipulate energy from evil-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to good divine servants or divine servants of good deities. …
evil.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Evil effects often manipulate energy from evil-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to good divine servants or divine servants of good deities. A creature with this trait is evil in alignment.

evocation.name: Evocation
evocation.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the evocation school of magic, typically involving energy and elemental forces.
evocation.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the evocation school of magic, typically involving energy and elemental forces.

exploration.name: Exploration
exploration.summary: An activity with this trait takes more than a turn to use, and can usually be used only during exploration mode.
exploration.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

An activity with this trait takes more than a turn to use, and can usually be used only during exploration mode.

extradimensional.name: Extradimensional
extradimensional.summary: This effect or item creates an extradimensional space. An extradimensional effect placed inside another extradimensional space ceases to function …
extradimensional.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

This effect or item creates an extradimensional space. An extradimensional effect placed inside another extradimensional space ceases to function until it is removed.

fear.name: Fear
fear.summary: Fear effects evoke the emotion of fear. Effects with this trait always have the  mental  and  emotion  traits as well.
fear.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Fear effects evoke the emotion of fear. Effects with this trait always have the mental and emotion traits as well.

fey.name: Fey
fey.summary: Creatures of the  First World  are called the fey.
fey.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Creatures of the First World are called the fey.

fiend.name: Fiend
fiend.summary: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the evil-aligned planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects …
fiend.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 631

Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the evil-aligned planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

fighter.name: Fighter
fighter.summary: This indicates abilities from the  fighter  class.
fighter.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

This indicates abilities from the fighter class.

fire.name: Fire
fire.summary: Effects with the fire trait deal fire damage or either conjure or manipulate fire. Those that manipulate fire have no effect in an area without fire. …
fire.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Effects with the fire trait deal fire damage or either conjure or manipulate fire. Those that manipulate fire have no effect in an area without fire. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of fire or have a magical connection to that element. Planes with this trait are composed of flames that continually burn with no fuel source. Fire planes are extremely hostile to non-fire creatures.

flourish.name: Flourish
flourish.summary: Flourish actions are actions that require too much exertion to perform a large number in a row. You can use only 1 action with the flourish trait per …
flourish.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Flourish actions are actions that require too much exertion to perform a large number in a row. You can use only 1 action with the flourish trait per turn.

focused.name: Focused
focused.summary: An item with this trait can give you an additional Focus Point. This focus point is separate from your focus pool and doesn’t count toward the cap on …
focused.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

An item with this trait can give you an additional Focus Point. This focus point is separate from your focus pool and doesn’t count toward the cap on your focus pool. You can gain this benefit only if you have a focus pool, and there might be restrictions on how the point can be used. You can’t gain more than 1 Focus Point per day from focused items.

force.name: Force
force.summary: Effects with this trait deal force damage or create objects made of pure magical force.
force.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Effects with this trait deal force damage or create objects made of pure magical force.

fortune.name: Fortune
fortune.summary: A fortune effect beneficially alters how you roll your dice. You can never have more than one fortune effect alter a single roll. If multiple fortune …
fortune.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A fortune effect beneficially alters how you roll your dice. You can never have more than one fortune effect alter a single roll. If multiple fortune effects would apply, you have to pick which to use. If a fortune effect and a misfortune effect would apply to the same roll, the two cancel each other out, and you roll normally.

fungus.name: Fungus
fungus.summary: Fungal creatures have the fungus trait. They are distinct from normal fungi.
fungus.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Fungal creatures have the fungus trait. They are distinct from normal fungi.

general.name: General
general.summary: A type of feat that any character can select, regardless of ancestry and class, as long as they meet the prerequisites. You can select a feat with …
general.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A type of feat that any character can select, regardless of ancestry and class, as long as they meet the prerequisites. You can select a feat with this trait when your class grants a general feat.

giant.name: Giant
giant.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Giants are massive humanoid creatures.

gnome.name: Gnome
gnome.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the gnome ancestry. Gnomes are small people skilled at magic who seek out new experiences and usually have  …
gnome.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A creature with this trait is a member of the gnome ancestry. Gnomes are small people skilled at magic who seek out new experiences and usually have low-light vision. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by gnomes. A weapon with this trait is created and used by gnomes.

goblin.name: Goblin
goblin.summary: A creature with this trait can be one of several kinds of creature, including  goblins ,  hobgoblins , and  bugbears . Goblins tend to have  …
goblin.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A creature with this trait can be one of several kinds of creature, including goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears. Goblins tend to have darkvision. An ability with this trait can be used or chosen only by goblins. A weapon with this trait is created and used by goblins.

good.name: Good
good.summary: Good effects often manipulate energy from good-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to evil divine servants or divine servants of evil deities. …
good.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

Good effects often manipulate energy from good-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to evil divine servants or divine servants of evil deities. A creature with this trait is good in alignment.

hag.name: Hag
hag.summary: These creatures are malevolent spellcasters who form covens.
hag.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

These creatures are malevolent spellcasters who form covens.

half-elf.name: Half-Elf
half-elf.summary: A creature with this trait is part  human  and part  elf . An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by half-elves.
half-elf.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A creature with this trait is part human and part elf. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by half-elves.

half-orc.name: Half-Orc
half-orc.summary: A creature with this trait is part  human  and part  orc . An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by half-orcs.
half-orc.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A creature with this trait is part human and part orc. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by half-orcs.

halfling.name: Halfling
halfling.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the  halfling  ancestry. These small people are friendly wanderers considered to be lucky. An ability with …
halfling.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A creature with this trait is a member of the halfling ancestry. These small people are friendly wanderers considered to be lucky. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by halflings. A weapon with this trait is created and used by halflings.

haunt.name: Haunt
haunt.summary: A hazard with this trait is a spiritual echo, often of someone with a tragic death. Putting a haunt to rest often involves resolving the haunt’s …
haunt.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A hazard with this trait is a spiritual echo, often of someone with a tragic death. Putting a haunt to rest often involves resolving the haunt’s unfinished business. A haunt that hasn’t been properly put to rest always returns after a time.

healing.name: Healing
healing.summary: A healing effect restores a creature's body, typically by restoring Hit Points, but sometimes by removing  diseases  or other debilitating effects.
healing.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 632

A healing effect restores a creature's body, typically by restoring Hit Points, but sometimes by removing diseases or other debilitating effects.

human.name: Human
human.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the  human  ancestry. Humans are a diverse array of people known for their adaptability. An ability with …
human.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

A creature with this trait is a member of the human ancestry. Humans are a diverse array of people known for their adaptability. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by humans.

humanoid.name: Humanoid
humanoid.summary: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.
humanoid.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

illusion.name: Illusion
illusion.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the illusion school of magic, typically involving false sensory stimuli.
illusion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the illusion school of magic, typically involving false sensory stimuli.

incapacitation.name: Incapacitation
incapacitation.summary: An ability with this trait can take a character completely out of the fight or even kill them, and it’s harder to use on a more powerful character. …
incapacitation.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

An ability with this trait can take a character completely out of the fight or even kill them, and it’s harder to use on a more powerful character. If a spell has the incapacitation trait, any creature of more than twice the spell’s level treats the result of their check to prevent being incapacitated by the spell as one degree of success better, or the result of any check the spellcaster made to incapacitate them as one degree of success worse. If any other effect has the incapacitation trait, a creature of higher level than the item, creature, or hazard generating the effect gains the same benefits.

infused.name: Infused
infused.summary: You created an  alchemical item  with the infused trait using your infused reagents, and it has a limited time before it becomes inert. Any …
infused.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 75

You created an alchemical item with the infused trait using your infused reagents, and it has a limited time before it becomes inert. Any nonpermanent effects from your infused alchemical items, with the exception of afflictions such as slow-acting poisons, end when you make your daily preparations again.

ingested.name: Ingested
ingested.summary: An ingested  poison  is activated by applying it to food or drink to be consumed by a living creature, or by placing it directly into a living …
ingested.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 550

An ingested poison is activated by applying it to food or drink to be consumed by a living creature, or by placing it directly into a living creature's mouth. A creature attempts a saving throw against such a poison when it consumes the poison or the food or drink treated with the poison. The onset time of ingested poisons typically ranges anywhere from 1 minute to 1 day.

inhaled.name: Inhaled
inhaled.summary: An inhaled  poison  is activated by unleashing it from its container. Once unleashed, the poison creates a cloud filling a 10-foot cube lasting for 1 …
inhaled.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 550

An inhaled poison is activated by unleashing it from its container. Once unleashed, the poison creates a cloud filling a 10-foot cube lasting for 1 minute or until a strong wind dissipates the cloud. Every creature entering this cloud is exposed to the poison and must attempt a saving throw against it; a creature aware of the poison before entering the cloud can use a single action to hold its breath and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the saving throw for 1 round.

injury.name: Injury
injury.summary: An injury  poison  is activated by applying it to a weapon or ammunition, and it affects the target of the first Strike made using the poisoned item. …
injury.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 550

An injury poison is activated by applying it to a weapon or ammunition, and it affects the target of the first Strike made using the poisoned item. If that Strike is a success and deals piercing or slashing damage, the target must attempt a saving throw against the poison. On a failed Strike, the target is unaffected, but the poison remains on the weapon and you can try again. On a critical failure, or if the Strike fails to deal slashing or piercing damage for some other reason, the poison is spent but the target is unaffected.

invested.name: Invested
invested.summary: A character can wear only 10 magical items that have the invested trait. None of the magical effects of the item apply if the character hasn’t …
invested.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

A character can wear only 10 magical items that have the invested trait. None of the magical effects of the item apply if the character hasn’t invested it, nor can it be activated, though the character still gains any normal benefits from wearing the physical item (like a hat keeping rain off their head).

lawful.name: Lawful
lawful.summary: Lawful effects often manipulate energy from law-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to chaotic divine servants or divine servants of chaotic …
lawful.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Lawful effects often manipulate energy from law-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to chaotic divine servants or divine servants of chaotic deities. A creature with this trait is lawful in alignment.

light.name: Light
light.summary: Light effects overcome non-magical darkness in the area, and can  counteract  magical  darkness . You must usually target darkness magic with your …
light.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Light effects overcome non-magical darkness in the area, and can counteract magical darkness. You must usually target darkness magic with your light magic directly to counteract the darkness, but some light spells automatically attempt to counteract darkness.

linguistic.name: Linguistic
linguistic.summary: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the …
linguistic.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

litany.name: Litany
litany.summary: Litanies are special devotion spells, typically used by champions and requiring a single action, that usually give temporary immunity to further …
litany.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Litanies are special devotion spells, typically used by champions and requiring a single action, that usually give temporary immunity to further litanies.

magical.name: Magical
magical.summary: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. A magical item radiates a magic aura …
magical.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. A magical item radiates a magic aura infused with its dominant school of magic.

 Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

manipulate.name: Manipulate
manipulate.summary: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform …
manipulate.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 633

You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

mechanical.name: Mechanical
mechanical.summary: A hazard with this trait is a constructed physical object.
mechanical.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

A hazard with this trait is a constructed physical object.

mental.name: Mental
mental.summary: A mental effect can alter the target's mind. It has no effect on an object or a  mindless  creature.
mental.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

A mental effect can alter the target's mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

metamagic.name: Metamagic
metamagic.summary: Actions with the metamagic trait, usually from metamagic feats, tweak the properties of your spells. You must use a metamagic action directly before …
metamagic.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Actions with the metamagic trait, usually from metamagic feats, tweak the properties of your spells. You must use a metamagic action directly before Casting the Spell you want to alter. If you use any action (including free actions and reactions) other than Cast a Spell directly after, you waste the benefits of the metamagic action. Any additional effects added by a metamagic action are part of the spell’s effect, not of the metamagic action itself.

mindless.name: Mindless
mindless.summary: A mindless creature has either programmed or rudimentary mental attributes. Most, if not all, of their mental ability modifiers are –5. They are …
mindless.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

A mindless creature has either programmed or rudimentary mental attributes. Most, if not all, of their mental ability modifiers are –5. They are immune to all mental effects.

minion.name: Minion
minion.summary: Minions are creatures that directly serve another creature. A creature with this trait can use only 2 actions per turn, doesn't have reactions, and …
minion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Minions are creatures that directly serve another creature. A creature with this trait can use only 2 actions per turn, doesn't have reactions, and can't act when it's not your turn. Your minion acts on your turn in combat, once per turn, when you spend an action to issue it commands. For an animal companion, you Command an Animal; for a minion that's a spell or magic item effect, like a summoned minion, you Sustain a Spell or Sustain an Activation; if not otherwise specified, you issue a verbal command as a single action with the auditory and concentrate traits. If given no commands, minions use no actions except to defend themselves or to escape obvious harm. If left unattended for long enough, typically 1 minute, mindless minions usually don't act, animals follow their instincts, and sapient minions act how they please. A minion can't control other creatures.

misfortune.name: Misfortune
misfortune.summary: A misfortune effect detrimentally alters how you roll your dice. You can never have more than one misfortune effect alter a single roll. If multiple …
misfortune.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

A misfortune effect detrimentally alters how you roll your dice. You can never have more than one misfortune effect alter a single roll. If multiple misfortune effects would apply, the GM decides which is worse and applies it. If a fortune effect and a misfortune effect would apply to the same roll, the two cancel each other out, and you roll normally.

monitor.name: Monitor
monitor.summary: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the neutrally aligned planes are called monitors. Monitors can survive the basic …
monitor.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the neutrally aligned planes are called monitors. Monitors can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

monk.name: Monk
monk.summary: Abilities with this trait are from the  monk  class. A weapon with this trait is primarily used by monks.
monk.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Abilities with this trait are from the monk class. A weapon with this trait is primarily used by monks.

morph.name: Morph
morph.summary: Effects that slightly alter a creature's form have the morph trait. Any Strikes specifically granted by a morph effect are magical. You can be …
morph.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Effects that slightly alter a creature's form have the morph trait. Any Strikes specifically granted by a morph effect are magical. You can be affected by multiple morph spells at once, but if you morph the same body part more than once, the second morph effect attempts to counteract the first (in the same manner as two polymorph effects, described in that trait).

Your morph effects might also end if you are polymorphed and the polymorph effect invalidates or overrides your morph effect. The GM determines which morph effects can be used together and which can't.

move.name: Move
move.summary: An action with this trait involves moving from one space to another.
move.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

An action with this trait involves moving from one space to another.

multiclass.name: Multiclass
multiclass.summary: Archetypes with the multiclass trait represent diversifying your training into another class’s specialties. You can’t select a multiclass archetype’s …
multiclass.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Archetypes with the multiclass trait represent diversifying your training into another class’s specialties. You can’t select a multiclass archetype’s dedication feat if you are a member of the class of the same name.

mutagen.name: Mutagen
mutagen.summary: An  elixir  with the mutagen trait temporarily transmogrifies the subject's body and alters its mind. A mutagen always conveys one or more beneficial …
mutagen.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

An elixir with the mutagen trait temporarily transmogrifies the subject's body and alters its mind. A mutagen always conveys one or more beneficial effects paired with one or more detrimental effects. Mutagens are polymorph effects, meaning you can benefit from only one at a time.

necromancy.name: Necromancy
necromancy.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the necromancy school of magic, typically involving forces of life and death.
necromancy.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the necromancy school of magic, typically involving forces of life and death.

negative.name: Negative
negative.summary: Effects with this trait heal  undead  creatures with negative energy, deal negative damage to living creatures, or manipulate negative energy. Planes …
negative.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Effects with this trait heal undead creatures with negative energy, deal negative damage to living creatures, or manipulate negative energy. Planes with this trait are vast, empty reaches that suck the life from the living. Creatures with this trait are natives of the Negative Energy Plane. They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Negative Energy Plane.

oath.name: Oath
oath.summary: Oaths add an additional tenet to your code. You can usually have only one feat with this trait.
oath.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 109

Oaths add an additional tenet to your code. You can usually have only one feat with this trait.

occult.name: Occult
occult.summary: This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is  magical .
occult.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.

oil.name: Oil
oil.summary: Oils are magical gels, ointments, pastes, or salves that are typically applied to an object and are used up in the process.
oil.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Oils are magical gels, ointments, pastes, or salves that are typically applied to an object and are used up in the process.

ooze.name: Ooze
ooze.summary: Oozes are creatures with simple anatomies. They tend to have low mental ability scores and immunity to  mental  effects and precision damage.
ooze.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

Oozes are creatures with simple anatomies. They tend to have low mental ability scores and immunity to mental effects and precision damage.

open.name: Open
open.summary: These maneuvers work only as the first salvo on your turn. You can use an open only if you haven't used an action with the  attack  or open trait yet …
open.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

These maneuvers work only as the first salvo on your turn. You can use an open only if you haven't used an action with the attack or open trait yet this turn.

orc.name: Orc
orc.summary: A creature with this trait is a member of the  orc  ancestry. These green-skinned people tend to have  darkvision . An ability with this trait can be …
orc.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 634

A creature with this trait is a member of the orc ancestry. These green-skinned people tend to have darkvision. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by orcs. An item with this trait is created and used by orcs.

plant.name: Plant
plant.summary: Vegetable creatures have the plant trait. They are distinct from normal plants. Magical effects with this trait manipulate or conjure plants or plant …
plant.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Vegetable creatures have the plant trait. They are distinct from normal plants. Magical effects with this trait manipulate or conjure plants or plant matter in some way. Those that manipulate plants have no effect in an area with no plants.

poison.name: Poison
poison.summary: An effect with this trait delivers a poison or deals poison damage. An item with this trait is poisonous and might cause an affliction.
poison.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

An effect with this trait delivers a poison or deals poison damage. An item with this trait is poisonous and might cause an affliction.

polymorph.name: Polymorph
polymorph.summary: These effects transform the target into a new form. A target can't be under the effect of more than one polymorph effect at a time. If it comes under …
polymorph.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

These effects transform the target into a new form. A target can't be under the effect of more than one polymorph effect at a time. If it comes under the effect of a second polymorph effect, the second polymorph effect attempts to counteract the first. If it succeeds, it takes effect, and if it fails, the spell has no effect on that target. Any Strikes specifically granted by a polymorph effect are magical. Unless otherwise stated, polymorph spells don't allow the target to take on the appearance of a specific individual creature, but rather just a generic creature of a general type or ancestry.

 If you take on a battle form with a polymorph spell, the special statistics can be adjusted only by circumstance bonuses, status bonuses, and penalties. Unless otherwise noted, the battle form prevents you from casting spells, speaking, and using most manipulate actions that require hands. (If there's doubt about whether you can use an action, the GM decides.) Your gear is absorbed into you; the constant abilities of your gear still function, but you can't activate any items.

positive.name: Positive
positive.summary: Effects with this trait heal living creatures with positive energy, deal positive energy damage to  undead , or manipulate positive energy. Planes …
positive.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Effects with this trait heal living creatures with positive energy, deal positive energy damage to undead, or manipulate positive energy. Planes with this trait are awash with life energy of such intensity that it can harm living creatures. Creatures with this trait are natives of the Positive Energy Plane. They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Positive Energy Plane.

possession.name: Possession
possession.summary: Effects with this trait allow a creature to project its mind and spirit into a target. A creature immune to  mental  effects can't use a possession …
possession.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Effects with this trait allow a creature to project its mind and spirit into a target. A creature immune to mental effects can't use a possession effect. While possessing a target, a possessor's true body is unconscious (and can't wake up normally), unless the possession effect allows the creature to physically enter the target. Whenever the target takes damage, the possessor takes half that amount of damage as mental damage.

 A possessor loses the benefits of any of its active spells or abilities that affect its physical body, though it gains the benefits of the target's active spells and abilities that affect their body. A possessor can use any of the target's abilities that are purely physical, and it can't use any of its own abilities except spells and purely mental abilities. The GM decides whether an ability is purely physical or purely mental. A possessor uses the target's attack modifier, AC, Fortitude save, Reflex save, Perception, and physical skills, and its own Will save, mental skills, spell attack roll, and spell DC; benefits of invested items apply where relevant (the possessor's invested items apply when using its own values, and the target's invested items apply when using the target's values). A possessor gains no benefit from casting spells that normally affect only the caster, since it isn't in its own body.

 The possessor must use its own actions to make the possessed creature act.

 If a possessor reaches 0 Hit Points through any combination of damage to its true body and mental damage from the possession, it is knocked out as normal and the possession immediately ends. If the target reaches 0 Hit Points first, the possessor can either fall unconscious with the body and continue the possession or end the effect as a free action and return to its body. If the target dies, the possession ends immediately and the possessor is stunned for 1 minute.

potion.name: Potion
potion.summary: A potion is a magical liquid activated when you drink it.
potion.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

A potion is a magical liquid activated when you drink it.

precious.name: Precious
precious.summary: Valuable materials with special properties have the precious trait. They can be substituted for base materials when you  Craft  items.
precious.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Valuable materials with special properties have the precious trait. They can be substituted for base materials when you Craft items.

prediction.name: Prediction
prediction.summary: Effects with this trait determine what is likely to happen in the near future. Most predictions are  divinations .
prediction.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Effects with this trait determine what is likely to happen in the near future. Most predictions are divinations.

press.name: Press
press.summary: Actions with this trait allow you to follow up earlier attacks. An action with the press trait can be used only if you are currently affected by a …
press.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Actions with this trait allow you to follow up earlier attacks. An action with the press trait can be used only if you are currently affected by a multiple attack penalty. Some actions with the press trait also grant an effect on a failure. The effects that are added on a failure don't apply on a critical failure. If your press action succeeds, you can choose to apply the failure effect instead. (For example, you may wish to do this when an attack deals no damage due to resistance.) Because a press action requires a multiple attack penalty, you can't use one when it's not your turn, even if you use the Ready activity.

primal.name: Primal
primal.summary: This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is  magical .
primal.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.

rage.name: Rage
rage.summary: You must be raging to use abilities with the rage trait, and they end automatically when you stop raging.
rage.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 87

You must be raging to use abilities with the rage trait, and they end automatically when you stop raging.

ranger.name: Ranger
ranger.summary: This indicates abilities from the  ranger  class.
ranger.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

This indicates abilities from the ranger class.

rare.name: Rare
rare.summary: This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players …
rare.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players only if the GM decides to include it in the game, typically through discovery during play. Creatures with this trait are rare. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creatures is increased by 5.

revelation.name: Revelation
revelation.summary: Effects with this trait see things as they truly are.
revelation.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

Effects with this trait see things as they truly are.

rogue.name: Rogue
rogue.summary: This indicates abilities from the  rogue  class.
rogue.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 635

This indicates abilities from the rogue class.

scroll.name: Scroll
scroll.summary: A scroll contains a single spell you can cast without a spell slot.
scroll.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

A scroll contains a single spell you can cast without a spell slot.

scrying.name: Scrying
scrying.summary: A scrying effect lets you see, hear, or otherwise get sensory information from a distance using a sensor or apparatus, rather than your own eyes and …
scrying.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

A scrying effect lets you see, hear, or otherwise get sensory information from a distance using a sensor or apparatus, rather than your own eyes and ears.

secret.name: Secret
secret.summary: The GM rolls the check for this ability in secret.
secret.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

The GM rolls the check for this ability in secret.

shadow.name: Shadow
shadow.summary: Magic with this trait involves shadows or the energy of the  Shadow Plane . Creatures with this trait are natives of the Shadow Plane. They can …
shadow.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

Magic with this trait involves shadows or the energy of the Shadow Plane. Creatures with this trait are natives of the Shadow Plane. They can survive the basic environmental effects of the Shadow Plane.

skill.name: Skill
skill.summary: A  general  feat with the skill trait improves your skills and their actions or gives you new actions for a skill. A feat with this trait can be …
skill.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

A general feat with the skill trait improves your skills and their actions or gives you new actions for a skill. A feat with this trait can be selected when a class grants a skill feat or general feat. Archetype feats with the skill trait can be selected in place of a skill feat if you have that archetype's dedication feat.

sleep.name: Sleep
sleep.summary: This effect can cause a creature to fall asleep or get drowsy.
sleep.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

This effect can cause a creature to fall asleep or get drowsy.

snare.name: Snare
snare.summary: Traps typically made by rangers, snares follow special rules that allow them to be constructed quickly and used on the battlefield.
snare.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

Traps typically made by rangers, snares follow special rules that allow them to be constructed quickly and used on the battlefield.

sonic.name: Sonic
sonic.summary: An effect with the sonic trait functions only if it makes sound, meaning it has no effect in an area of silence or in a vacuum. This is different …
sonic.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

An effect with the sonic trait functions only if it makes sound, meaning it has no effect in an area of silence or in a vacuum. This is different from an auditory spell, which is effective only if the target can hear it. A sonic effect might deal sonic damage. A creature with this trait has a magical connection to powerful sound.

sorcerer.name: Sorcerer
sorcerer.summary: This indicates abilities from the  sorcerer  class.
sorcerer.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

This indicates abilities from the sorcerer class.

spirit.name: Spirit
spirit.summary: Spirits are ephemeral creatures defined by their spiritual self and often lacking a physical form.
spirit.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 636

Spirits are ephemeral creatures defined by their spiritual self and often lacking a physical form.

splash.name: Splash
splash.summary: When you use a thrown weapon with the splash trait, you don’t add your Strength modifier to the damage roll. If an attack with a splash weapon fails, …
splash.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

When you use a thrown weapon with the splash trait, you don’t add your Strength modifier to the damage roll. If an attack with a splash weapon fails, succeeds, or critically succeeds, all creatures within 5 feet of the target (including the target) take the listed splash damage. On a failure (but not a critical failure), the target of the attack still takes the splash damage. Add splash damage together with the initial damage against the target before applying the target’s weaknesses or resistances. You don’t multiply splash damage on a critical hit.

staff.name: Staff
staff.summary: This magic item holds spells of a particular theme and allows a spellcaster to cast additional spells by preparing the staff.
staff.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

This magic item holds spells of a particular theme and allows a spellcaster to cast additional spells by preparing the staff.

stance.name: Stance
stance.summary: A stance is a general combat strategy that you enter by using an action with the stance trait, and that you remain in for some time. A stance lasts …
stance.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

A stance is a general combat strategy that you enter by using an action with the stance trait, and that you remain in for some time. A stance lasts until you get knocked out, until its requirements (if any) are violated, until the encounter ends, or until you enter a new stance, whichever comes first. After you use an action with the stance trait, you can’t use another one for 1 round. You can enter or be in a stance only in encounter mode.

structure.name: Structure
structure.summary: An item with the structure trait creates a magical building or other structure when activated. The item must be activated on a plot of land free of …
structure.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 596

An item with the structure trait creates a magical building or other structure when activated. The item must be activated on a plot of land free of other structures. The structure adapts to the natural terrain, adopting the structural requirements for being built there. The structure adjusts around small features such as ponds or spires of rock, but it can’t be created on water or other nonsolid surfaces. If activated on snow, sand dunes, or other soft surfaces with a solid surface underneath, the structure’s foundation (if any) reaches the solid ground. If an item with this trait is activated on a solid but unstable surface, such as a swamp or an area plagued by tremors, roll a DC 3 flat check each day; on a failure, the structure begins to sink or collapse.

 The structure doesn’t harm creatures within the area when it appears, and it can’t be created within a crowd or in a densely populated area. Any creature inadvertently caught inside the structure when the item is activated ends up unharmed inside the complete structure and always has a clear path of escape. A creature inside the structure when the activation ends isn’t harmed, and it lands harmlessly on the ground if it was on an upper level of the structure.

summoned.name: Summoned
summoned.summary: A creature called by a spell or effect gains the summoned trait. A summoned creature can't summon other creatures, create things of value, or cast …
summoned.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

A creature called by a spell or effect gains the summoned trait. A summoned creature can't summon other creatures, create things of value, or cast spells that require a cost. It has the minion trait. If it tries to Cast a Spell of equal or higher level than the spell that summoned it, it overpowers the summoning magic, causing its own spell to fail and the summon spell to end. Otherwise, the summoned creature uses the standard abilities for a creature of its kind. It generally attacks your enemies to the best of its ability. If you can communicate with it, you can attempt to command it, but the GM determines the degree to which it follows your commands.

 Immediately when you finish Casting the Spell, the summoned creature uses its 2 actions for that turn. A spawn or other creature generated from a summoned creature returns to its unaltered state (usually a corpse in the case of spawn) once the summoned creature is gone. If it's unclear what this state would be, the GM decides. Summoned creatures can be banished by various spells and effects. They are automatically banished if reduced to 0 Hit Points or if the spell that called them ends.

talisman.name: Talisman
talisman.summary: A talisman is a small object affixed to armor, a shield, or a weapon (called the affixed item). You must be wielding or wearing an item to activate a …
talisman.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

A talisman is a small object affixed to armor, a shield, or a weapon (called the affixed item). You must be wielding or wearing an item to activate a talisman attached to it. Once activated, a talisman burns out permanently.

teleportation.name: Teleportation
teleportation.summary: Teleportation effects allow you to instantaneously move from one point in space to another. Teleportation does not usually trigger reactions based on …
teleportation.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

Teleportation effects allow you to instantaneously move from one point in space to another. Teleportation does not usually trigger reactions based on movement.

transmutation.name: Transmutation
transmutation.summary: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the transmutation school of magic, typically changing something’s form.
transmutation.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the transmutation school of magic, typically changing something’s form.

trap.name: Trap
trap.summary: A hazard or item with this trait is constructed to hinder interlopers.
trap.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

A hazard or item with this trait is constructed to hinder interlopers.

uncommon.name: Uncommon
uncommon.summary: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to …
uncommon.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

undead.name: Undead
undead.summary: Once living, these creatures were infused after death with negative energy and soul-corrupting evil magic. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, an undead …
undead.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

Once living, these creatures were infused after death with negative energy and soul-corrupting evil magic. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, an undead creature is destroyed. Undead creatures are damaged by positive energy, are healed by negative energy, and don't benefit from healing effects.

unique.name: Unique
unique.summary: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of  Recall Knowledge  checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.
unique.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 637

A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

virulent.name: Virulent
virulent.summary: Afflictions with the virulent trait are harder to remove. You must succeed at two consecutive saves to reduce a virulent affliction’s stage by 1. A …
virulent.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 638

Afflictions with the virulent trait are harder to remove. You must succeed at two consecutive saves to reduce a virulent affliction’s stage by 1. A critical success reduces a virulent affliction’s stage by only 1 instead of by 2.

visual.name: Visual
visual.summary: A visual effect can affect only creatures that can see it. This applies only to visible parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.
visual.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 638

A visual effect can affect only creatures that can see it. This applies only to visible parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.

wand.name: Wand
wand.summary: A wand contains a single spell which you can cast once per day.
wand.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 638

A wand contains a single spell which you can cast once per day.

water.name: Water
water.summary: Effects with the water trait either manipulate or conjure water. Those that manipulate water have no effect in an area without water. Creatures with …
water.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 638

Effects with the water trait either manipulate or conjure water. Those that manipulate water have no effect in an area without water. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of water or have a magical connection to the element. Planes with this trait are mostly liquid, perhaps with pockets of breathable air.

wizard.name: Wizard
wizard.summary: This indicates abilities from the  wizard  class.
wizard.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 638

This indicates abilities from the wizard class.

aquatic.name: Aquatic
aquatic.summary: Aquatic creatures are at home underwater. Their bludgeoning and slashing unarmed Strikes don’t take the usual –2 penalty for being underwater. …
aquatic.description:
**Source** Bestiary pg. 345

Aquatic creatures are at home underwater. Their bludgeoning and slashing unarmed Strikes don’t take the usual –2 penalty for being underwater. Aquatic creatures can breathe water but not air.

agile.name: Agile
agile.summary: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and …
agile.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

attached.name: Attached
attached.summary: An attached weapon must be combined with another piece of gear to be used. The trait lists what type of item the weapon must be attached to. You must …
attached.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

An attached weapon must be combined with another piece of gear to be used. The trait lists what type of item the weapon must be attached to. You must be wielding or wearing the item the weapon is attached to in order to attack with it. For example, shield spikes are attached to a shield, allowing you to attack with the spikes instead of a shield bash, but only if you're wielding the shield. An attached weapon is usually bolted onto or built into the item it's attached to, and typically an item can have only one weapon attached to it. An attached weapon can be affixed to an item with 10 minutes of work and a successful DC 10 Crafting check; this includes the time needed to remove the weapon from a previous item, if necessary. If an item is destroyed, its attached weapon can usually be salvaged.

backstabber.name: Backstabber
backstabber.summary: When you hit a  flat-footed  creature, this weapon deals 1 precision damage in addition to its normal damage. The precision damage increases to 2 if …
backstabber.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

When you hit a flat-footed creature, this weapon deals 1 precision damage in addition to its normal damage. The precision damage increases to 2 if the weapon is a _+3 weapon_.

backswing.name: Backswing
backswing.summary: You can use the momentum from a missed attack with this weapon to lead into your next attack. After missing with this weapon on your turn, you gain a …
backswing.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

You can use the momentum from a missed attack with this weapon to lead into your next attack. After missing with this weapon on your turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your next attack with this weapon before the end of your turn.

deadly.name: Deadly
deadly.summary: On a critical hit, the weapon adds a weapon damage die of the listed size. Roll this after doubling the weapon's damage. This increases to two dice …
deadly.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

On a critical hit, the weapon adds a weapon damage die of the listed size. Roll this after doubling the weapon's damage. This increases to two dice if the weapon has a _greater striking rune_ and three dice if the weapon has a _major striking rune_. For instance, a rapier with a _greater striking rune_ deals 2d8 extra piercing damage on a critical hit. An ability that changes the size of the weapon's normal damage dice doesn't change the size of its deadly die.

disarm.name: Disarm
disarm.summary: You can use this weapon to  Disarm  with the  Athletics  skill even if you don't have a free hand. This uses the weapon's  reach  (if different from …
disarm.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

You can use this weapon to Disarm with the Athletics skill even if you don't have a free hand. This uses the weapon's reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls (if any) as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Disarm using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure. On a critical success, you still need a free hand if you want to take the item.

fatal.name: Fatal
fatal.summary: The fatal trait includes a die size. On a critical hit, the weapon’s damage die increases to that die size instead of the normal die size, and the …
fatal.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

The fatal trait includes a die size. On a critical hit, the weapon’s damage die increases to that die size instead of the normal die size, and the weapon adds one additional damage die of the listed size.

finesse.name: Finesse
finesse.summary: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still use your Strength modifier …
finesse.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still use your Strength modifier when calculating damage.

forceful.name: Forceful
forceful.summary: This weapon becomes more dangerous as you build momentum. When you attack with it more than once on your turn, the second attack gains a circumstance …
forceful.description:
**Source** Core Rulebook pg. 282

This weapon becomes more dangerous as you build momentum. When you attack with it more than once on your turn, the second attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice, and each subsequent attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.

free-hand.name: Free-Hand
free-hand.summary: This weapon doesn't take up your hand, usually because it is built into your armor. A free-hand weapon can't be  Disarmed . You can use the hand …
free-hand.description:
